mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above trend signals a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment below 5% signals a labor market tightening that compresses wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in labor market tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 2,000 signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4% signals a monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in monetary tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 130,000 signals a labor market expansion that boosts consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor market expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 11,000 signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above trend and a 3-month increase in housing starts above 2,000 signals a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 3-month moving average and HOUST rises above 2,000
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
